generatedSentence: A dystopian future. Elias, a seasoned librarian and a weary bloke of quiet wisdom, carried his worn knapsack through the decaying city. He made a quiet remark about the perpetually matte sky. His daily routine felt ordinary, a monthly ritual. He remembered a time when the earth was vibrant, not shrouded in a haze that seemed to opacify all hope. In his small holt, a humble dwelling, he kept a secret, a single wafer of ancient tech, a prototype of an inexhaustible data core. The colossal boiler powering the central warehouse hummed ceaselessly. He felt a deep guilt for the lost past, a heavy weight like kraut. A sliver of an idea, an inkling of something unreal, a faint tinder of rebellion, always lingered. His resolve, however, was bold. One night, a peculiar anomaly appeared on his archaic browser: a kanji symbol he'd never seen, linked to a hidden harbour of knowledge. This was the problem he'd sensed, a dangerous theme the rulers tried to boycott. He knew the admirals of this iron rule, their opulent lifestyle built on the suffering majority, their narcotics keeping the populace numb. He felt a fierce loyalty to the truth. A grotesque image of a griffon carved into an obelisk on the data page gave him a knock of dread. He imagined them knuckling down on anyone who dared to question their monotheism of technology. He saw a snake in the system, a system that felt like laundering away all true information. His lipsync to the daily propaganda was becoming harder. Whenever he thought of the truth, he felt a powerful kicking in his chest. He uncovered a vast wreckage of suppressed information. Elias, summoning all his willpower, decided to act. He knew the risks; an oncologist friend had secretly shown him the horrifying effects of the noise pollution on the population's cholesterol levels, a direct result of the city's power source. He felt doubly motivated. He had to make an improvement. He took a tank of precious cider – a liquid that could temporarily bewitch the city's sensors – and made his way to the central doorway of the immense data vault, which resembled a broken boiler. His heart was winded, but his resolve was clear. The vault was protected by a jury of security protocols. He imagined the walrus-like guards inside, their faces like polished bronze. He was starting a fight against an ogre of an organization. His jumper was soaked with nervous sweat. He remembered the old legends of heaven and the universe, things they now called unreal. His actions felt fiercely important, a dipping into the unknown. He knew the very elements of reality were at stake. After an intense iteration of bypassing defenses, Elias successfully uploaded a counter-program. A single dollar deposit of truth into the system. The screens inside the vault, previously displaying only orthogonal propaganda, now shimmered with true information. He felt a rush of fulfilling satisfaction. He watched, almost in a trance, as the information flowed like juicy nectar, a sweet secretion of forgotten facts. The city's veiled healthcare system, its true costs, and the extent of its manufactured shortages were laid bare for the majority. He felt a rejoice within him. He saw images of free macaws flying over vast, clean oceans, a vision of wonderland the rulers had denied. His mission, his "ouroboros" of truth returning, was complete. He smiled, a veracity in his lips that had been absent for years. He took a bucket of the corrupted data and watched it melt like glass in the dawn's cold winter light, as if a cosmic sweeper had cleansed the system. He even saw a llama graze on newly greened earth, a marvellous sign. The future, once bleak, now held an infinite kalpa of possibilities. His wardrobe felt lighter, a true transformation. His wraps tightened, a symbol of a new era.
panelSentences: A dystopian future. Elias, a seasoned librarian and a weary bloke of quiet wisdom, carried his worn knapsack through the decaying city. He made a quiet remark about the perpetually matte sky. His daily routine felt ordinary, a monthly ritual. He remembered a time when the earth was vibrant, not shrouded in a haze that seemed to opacify all hope. In his small holt, a humble dwelling, he kept a secret, a single wafer of ancient tech, a prototype of an inexhaustible data core. The colossal boiler powering the central warehouse hummed ceaselessly. He felt a deep guilt for the lost past, a heavy weight like kraut. A sliver of an idea, an inkling of something unreal, a faint tinder of rebellion, always lingered. His resolve, however, was bold.

One night, a peculiar anomaly appeared on his archaic browser: a kanji symbol he'd never seen, linked to a hidden harbour of knowledge. This was the problem he'd sensed, a dangerous theme the rulers tried to boycott. He knew the admirals of this iron rule, their opulent lifestyle built on the suffering majority, their narcotics keeping the populace numb. He felt a fierce loyalty to the truth. A grotesque image of a griffon carved into an obelisk on the data page gave him a knock of dread. He imagined them knuckling down on anyone who dared to question their monotheism of technology. He saw a snake in the system, a system that felt like laundering away all true information. His lipsync to the daily propaganda was becoming harder. Whenever he thought of the truth, he felt a powerful kicking in his chest. He uncovered a vast wreckage of suppressed information.

Elias, summoning all his willpower, decided to act. He knew the risks; an oncologist friend had secretly shown him the horrifying effects of the noise pollution on the population's cholesterol levels, a direct result of the city's power source. He felt doubly motivated. He had to make an improvement. He took a tank of precious cider – a liquid that could temporarily bewitch the city's sensors – and made his way to the central doorway of the immense data vault, which resembled a broken boiler. His heart was winded, but his resolve was clear. The vault was protected by a jury of security protocols. He imagined the walrus-like guards inside, their faces like polished bronze. He was starting a fight against an ogre of an organization. His jumper was soaked with nervous sweat. He remembered the old legends of heaven and the universe, things they now called unreal. His actions felt fiercely important, a dipping into the unknown. He knew the very elements of reality were at stake.

After an intense iteration of bypassing defenses, Elias successfully uploaded a counter-program. A single dollar deposit of truth into the system. The screens inside the vault, previously displaying only orthogonal propaganda, now shimmered with true information. He felt a rush of fulfilling satisfaction. He watched, almost in a trance, as the information flowed like juicy nectar, a sweet secretion of forgotten facts. The city's veiled healthcare system, its true costs, and the extent of its manufactured shortages were laid bare for the majority. He felt a rejoice within him. He saw images of free macaws flying over vast, clean oceans, a vision of wonderland the rulers had denied. His mission, his "ouroboros" of truth returning, was complete. He smiled, a veracity in his lips that had been absent for years. He took a bucket of the corrupted data and watched it melt like glass in the dawn's cold winter light, as if a cosmic sweeper had cleansed the system. He even saw a llama graze on newly greened earth, a marvellous sign. The future, once bleak, now held an infinite kalpa of possibilities. His wardrobe felt lighter, a true transformation. His wraps tightened, a symbol of a new era.
rawPanelSentences: A **dystopian** future. Elias, a seasoned **librarian** and a weary **bloke** of quiet **wisdom**, carried his worn **knapsack** through the decaying city. He made a quiet **remark** about the perpetually **matte** sky. His daily routine felt **ordinary**, a **monthly** ritual. He remembered a time when the **earth** was vibrant, not shrouded in a haze that seemed to **opacify** all hope. In his small **holt**, a **humble** dwelling, he kept a secret, a single **wafer** of ancient tech, a **prototype** of an **inexhaustible** data core. The colossal **boiler** powering the central **warehouse** hummed ceaselessly. He felt a deep **guilt** for the lost past, a heavy weight like **kraut**. A **sliver** of an idea, an **inkling** of something **unreal**, a faint **tinder** of rebellion, always lingered. His resolve, however, was **bold**.

One **night**, a peculiar **anomaly** appeared on his archaic **browser**: a **kanji** symbol he'd never seen, linked to a hidden **harbour** of knowledge. This was the **problem** he'd sensed, a dangerous **theme** the rulers tried to **boycott**. He knew the **admirals** of this iron rule, their **opulent** lifestyle built on the suffering **majority**, their **narcotics** keeping the populace numb. He felt a fierce **loyalty** to the truth. A **grotesque** image of a **griffon** carved into an **obelisk** on the data page gave him a **knock** of dread. He imagined them **knuckling** down on anyone who dared to question their **monotheism** of technology. He saw a **snake** in the system, a system that felt like **laundering** away all true information. His **lipsync** to the daily propaganda was becoming harder. **Whenever** he thought of the truth, he felt a powerful **kicking** in his chest. He uncovered a vast **wreckage** of suppressed information.

Elias, summoning all his **willpower**, decided to act. He knew the risks; an **oncologist** friend had secretly shown him the horrifying effects of the **noise** pollution on the population's **cholesterol** levels, a direct result of the city's power source. He felt **doubly** motivated. He had to make an **improvement**. He took a **tank** of precious **cider** – a liquid that could temporarily **bewitch** the city's sensors – and made his way to the central **doorway** of the immense data vault, which resembled a **broken** **boiler**. His heart was **winded**, but his resolve was clear. The vault was protected by a **jury** of security protocols. He imagined the **walrus**-like guards inside, their faces like polished **bronze**. He was **starting** a fight against an **ogre** of an organization. His **jumper** was soaked with nervous sweat. He remembered the old legends of **heaven** and the **universe**, things they now called **unreal**. His actions felt **fiercely** important, a **dipping** into the unknown. He knew the very **elements** of reality were at stake.

After an intense **iteration** of bypassing defenses, Elias successfully uploaded a counter-program. A single **dollar** **deposit** of truth into the system. The screens inside the vault, previously displaying only **orthogonal** propaganda, now shimmered with true information. He felt a rush of **fulfilling** satisfaction. He watched, almost in a trance, as the information flowed like **juicy** nectar, a sweet **secretion** of forgotten facts. The city's veiled **healthcare** system, its true costs, and the extent of its manufactured shortages were laid bare for the **majority**. He felt a **rejoice** within him. He saw images of free **macaws** flying over vast, clean **oceans**, a vision of **wonderland** the rulers had denied. His mission, his "**ouroboros**" of truth returning, was complete. He smiled, a **veracity** in his **lips** that had been absent for years. He took a **bucket** of the corrupted data and watched it melt like **glass** in the dawn's cold **winter** light, as if a cosmic **sweeper** had cleansed the system. He even saw a **llama** graze on newly greened earth, a **marvellous** sign. The future, once bleak, now held an infinite **kalpa** of possibilities. His **wardrobe** felt lighter, a true transformation. His **wraps** tightened, a symbol of a new era.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, modern neo-noir style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. The protagonist is a grizzled man in his late 50s, with a prominent five o'clock shadow and deep-set eyes, wearing a simple, patched jacket. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elias, the grizzled man, walking through a dimly lit, rain-slicked dystopian city street, holding a worn knapsack, cityscape towers looming in the background, low-angle shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elias, the grizzled man, hunched over an old, glowing computer screen displaying strange symbols and a hidden map, his face illuminated by the screen, close-up on his focused, wrinkled face. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elias, the grizzled man, stealthily approaching a massive, imposing, broken-down data vault door, holding a glowing vial of liquid, security cameras visible in the distance, medium shot from behind. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elias, the grizzled man, standing confidently on a rooftop overlooking a now brightly lit city skyline, a clean llama grazing on a patch of newly greened grass nearby, colorful macaws flying in the distance, a wide, triumphant shot. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, all humans visibly adult with mature features.

definitions.definition: An act of pointing out or noticing; notice or observation.
An expression, in speech or writing, of something remarked or noticed; a mention of something
definitions.example: a biting remark

definitions.definition: A casual observation, comment, or statement
To make a remark or remarks; to comment.
To express in words or writing; to state; to make a comment
definitions.example: He remarked that it was getting late.

definitions.definition: To pay heed to; notice; to take notice of
To mark in a notable manner; to distinguish clearly; to make noticeable or conspicuous; to point out.
(engraving) A small design etched on the margin of a plate and supposed to be removed after the earliest proofs have been taken.
(engraving) Any feature distinguishing a particular stage of the plate.
(engraving) A print or proof distinguished by such a mark.
The keeper, manager of a library.
One who cares for the publications, files etc. in a library, whether staff or volunteer.
A person who processes and organizes information.
A case of canvas or leather, for carrying items on the back.
A set of values from which a subset is chosen.
To go hiking while burdened with a knapsack, usually overnight or for longer.
definitions.example: My sleeping bag fell off my backpack into the water, while we were knapsacking up the mountain.

definitions.definition: A dish made by fermenting finely chopped cabbage.
A German person.
A dwelling; habitation; building.
The act of boycotting.
To abstain, either as an individual or a group, from using, buying, or dealing with someone or some organization as an expression of protest.
To cast a spell upon.
To fascinate or charm.
definitions.example: I was bewitched by the sight of the girl dancing in the forest.

definitions.definition: To astonish, amaze.
A naturally occurring or man-made alloy of copper, usually in combination with tin, but also with one or more other metals.
A reddish-brown colour, the colour of bronze.
A work of art made of bronze, especially a sculpture.
A bronze medal.
definitions.example: She wanted to win the tournament, but had to settle for the bronze after being beaten in the semi-finals.

definitions.definition: Boldness; impudence; brass.
To plate with bronze.
definitions.example: My mother bronzed my first pair of baby shoes.

definitions.definition: To color bronze; (of the sun) to tan.
(of the skin) To change to a bronze or tan colour due to exposure to the sun.
To make hard or unfeeling; to brazen.
Made of bronze metal.
Having a reddish-brown colour.
(of the skin) Tanned; darkened as a result of exposure to the sun.
A person who boils something.
A steam boiler.
An apparatus for heating circulating water or other heat transferring liquid.
A device consisting of a heat source and a tank for storing hot water, typically for space heating, domestic hot water etc., disregarding the source of heat.
A kitchen vessel for steaming, boiling or heating food.
A sunken reef, especially a coral reef, on which the sea breaks heavily.
A tough old chicken only suitable for cooking by boiling.
A mythical beast having the body of a lion and the wings and head of an eagle.
A large vulture (Gyps fulvus) found in the mountainous parts of Southern Europe, North Africa, and Asia Minor, supposed to be the "eagle" of the Bible.
An English variety of apple.
A person who has just arrived from Europe.
A cadet newly arrived in British India: half English, half Indian.
A watchful guardian, especially a duenna in charge of a young woman.
A breed of dog usually called the Brussels Griffon, reminiscent of the legendary creature.
A griffon vulture.
Something wrecked, especially the remains or debris of something which has been severely damaged or destroyed.
A light, thin, flat biscuit/cookie.
(christianity) A thin disk of consecrated unleavened bread used in communion.
A soft disk originally made of flour, and later of gelatin or a similar substance, used to seal letters, attach papers etc.
A thin disk of silicon or other semiconductor on which an electronic circuit is produced.
To seal or fasten with a wafer.
(interrogative) When ever: emphatic form of when.
definitions.example: Whenever will I find time to finish that jigsaw?

definitions.definition: At any time.
definitions.example: Feel free to visit whenever.

definitions.definition: At any time that.
definitions.example: Visit whenever you want to.

definitions.definition: At the (single) time that, no matter when.
definitions.example: Tomorrow I'll get up whenever the sun rises.

definitions.definition: Every time that.
definitions.example: Whenever he has a pair of aces, his eyelids twitch.

definitions.definition: Regardless of the time that.
definitions.example: Income must be reported in the period in which it is earned, whenever payment is actually received.

definitions.definition: When.
definitions.example: Whenever I was a child, I lived in Arkansas.

definitions.definition: In a fierce manner.
definitions.example: The wind blew fiercely and the rain fell heavily.

definitions.definition: The third planet of the Solar System; the world upon which humans live.
The personification of the Earth or earth, as a fertile woman or goddess.
Soil.
definitions.example: This is good earth for growing potatoes.

definitions.definition: Any general rock-based material.
definitions.example: She sighed when the plane's wheels finally touched earth.

definitions.definition: The ground, land (as opposed to the sky or sea).
definitions.example: Birds are of the sky, not of the earth.

definitions.definition: A connection electrically to the earth ((US) ground); on equipment: a terminal connected in that manner.
The lair (as a hole on the ground) of an animal such as fox.
A region of the planet; a land or country.
Worldly things, as against spiritual ones.
The world of our current life (as opposed to heaven or an afterlife).
The people on the globe.
The human body.
The aforementioned soil- or rock-based material, considered one of the four or five classical elements.
Any of certain substances now known to be oxides of metal, which were distinguished by being infusible, and by insolubility in water.
To connect electrically to the earth.
definitions.example: That noise is because the amplifier is not properly earthed.

definitions.definition: To bury.
To burrow.
Impossible to exhaust; unlimited.
Recital or performance a second time; repetition.
A variation or version.
definitions.example: The architect drafted several iterations of the floorplan before deciding on his final design.

definitions.definition: The use of repetition in a computer program, especially in the form of a loop.
A single repetition of the code within such a repetitive process.
definitions.example: The code calculates the appropriate value at each iteration.

definitions.definition: Any place of shelter.
definitions.example: The neighborhood is a well-known harbor for petty thieves.

definitions.definition: A sheltered expanse of water, adjacent to land, in which ships may anchor or dock, especially for loading and unloading.
(glassworking) A mixing box for materials.
A house of the zodiac, or the mansion of a heavenly body.
Shelter, refuge.
To provide a harbor or safe place for.
definitions.example: The docks, which once harbored tall ships, now harbor only petty thieves.

definitions.definition: To take refuge or shelter in a protected expanse of water.
definitions.example: The fleet harbored in the south.

definitions.definition: To drive (a hunted stag) to covert.
To hold or persistently entertain in one's thoughts or mind.
definitions.example: She harbors a conviction that her husband has a secret, criminal past.

definitions.definition: The prevention, treatment, and management of illness or the preservation of mental and physical well-being through the services offered by the medical, nursing, and allied health professions.
definitions.example: I think free health care should also cover nearsightedness.

definitions.definition: To make opaque.
A type of brutish giant from folk tales that eats human flesh.
A brutish man reminiscent of the mythical ogre.
Luxuriant, and ostentatiously magnificent.
Rich, sumptuous and extravagant.
The distinctive scent, caused by geosmin, which accompanies the first rain after a long, warm, dry spell.
The yellow organic oil that yields this scent.
Of or pertaining to a dystopia.
Dire; characterized by human suffering or misery.
Sediment or rock that is not native to its present location or is different from the surrounding material. Sometimes refers to ore or gems.
That which is placed anywhere, or in anyone's hands, for safekeeping; something entrusted to the care of another.
Money placed in an account.
Anything left behind on a surface.
definitions.example: a deposit of seaweed on the shore

definitions.definition: A sum of money or other asset given as an initial payment, to show good faith, or to reserve something for purchase.
definitions.example: They put a deposit on the apartment.

definitions.definition: A sum of money given as a security for a borrowed item, which will be given back when the item is returned, e.g. a bottle deposit or can deposit
A place of deposit; a depository.
To lay down; to place; to put.
definitions.example: A crocodile deposits her eggs in the sand.

definitions.definition: To lay up or away for safekeeping; to put up; to store.
definitions.example: to deposit goods in a warehouse

definitions.definition: To entrust one's assets to the care of another. Sometimes done as collateral.
To put money or funds into an account.
To lay aside; to rid oneself of.
To lower into a liquid.
definitions.example: Dip your biscuit into your tea.

definitions.definition: To immerse oneself; to become plunged in a liquid; to sink.
(of a value or rate) To decrease slightly.
To lower a light's beam.
definitions.example: Dip your lights as you meet an oncoming car.

definitions.definition: To lower (a flag), particularly a national ensign, to a partially hoisted position in order to render or to return a salute. While lowered, the flag is said to be “at the dip.” A flag being carried on a staff may be dipped by leaning it forward at an approximate angle of 45 degrees.
definitions.example: “The sailor rushed to the flag hoist to dip the flag in return.”

definitions.definition: To treat cattle or sheep by immersion in chemical solution.
definitions.example: The farmer is going to dip the cattle today.

definitions.definition: To use a dip stick to check oil level in an engine.
To consume snuff by placing a pinch behind the lip or under the tongue so that the active chemical constituents of the snuff may be absorbed into the system for their narcotic effect.
To immerse for baptism.
To wet, as if by immersing; to moisten.
To plunge or engage thoroughly in any affair.
To take out, by dipping a dipper, ladle, or other receptacle, into a fluid and removing a part; often with out.
definitions.example: to dip water from a boiler; to dip out water

definitions.definition: To perform the action of plunging a dipper, ladle. etc. into a liquid or soft substance and removing a part.
To engage as a pledge; to mortgage.
To perform (a bow or curtsey) by inclining the body.
To incline downward from the plane of the horizon.
definitions.example: Strata of rock dip.

definitions.definition: To perform a dip dance move (often phrased with the leader as the subject noun and the follower as the subject noun being dipped)
To lower the body by bending the knees while keeping the body in an upright position, as in movement to the rhythm of music.
To leave.
definitions.example: He dipped out of the room so fast.

definitions.definition: To miss out on seeing a sought after bird.
An act or process of immersing.
The act of inclining downward.
The act of lifting or moving a liquid with a dipper, ladle, or the like.
The process of cleaning or brightening sheet metal or metalware, especially brass, by dipping it in acids, etc.
The use of dipping tobacco (moist snuff) in the mouth, usually between the lip and gum or cheek and gum in the lower or upper part of the mouth.
The act or fact of missing out on seeing a bird.
A deviation from a rule or from what is regarded as normal; an outlier.
Something or someone that is strange or unusual.
definitions.example: He is an anomaly among his friends in that he's the only one who's unmarried.

definitions.definition: Any event or measurement that is out of the ordinary regardless of whether it is exceptional or not.
definitions.example: She disregarded some of the anamolies in the experiment, putting them down to miscalculation.

definitions.definition: Any of various angular distances.
A defect or malformation.
A failure of a classical symmetry due to quantum corrections.
An irregularity or disproportion.
A long piece cut or rent off; a sharp, slender fragment; a splinter.
A strand, or slender roll, of cotton or other fiber in a loose, untwisted state, produced by a carding machine and ready for the roving or slubbing which precedes spinning.
Bait made of pieces of small fish. Compare kibblings.
A narrow high-rise apartment building.
To cut or divide into long, thin pieces, or into very small pieces; to cut or rend lengthwise; to slit.
definitions.example: to sliver wood

definitions.definition: Small dry sticks and finely-divided fibrous matter etc., used to help light a fire.
To set fire to; torch.
To wash; to wash, and to smooth with a flatiron or mangle; to wash and iron.
To lave; to wet.
(money) To disguise the source of (ill-gotten wealth) by various means.
The act, or occupation, of one who launders; washing and ironing.
The synchronization of prerecorded audio signals and movement of the lips such that there is no lack of simultaneity between them.
To move lips in synchronization with sounds (generally recorded) so it appears they are the source of the sounds.
definitions.example: She lip-synced to her own recording during the show because of her laryngitis.

definitions.definition: To apply pressure, or rub or massage with one's knuckles.
definitions.example: He knuckled the sleep from his eyes.

definitions.definition: To bend the fingers.
To touch one's forehead as a mark of respect.
To yield.
Responsibility for wrongdoing.
The state of having been found guilty or admitted guilt in legal proceedings.
The regret of having done wrong.
A style of ornamentation characterized by fanciful combinations of intertwined forms.
Anything grotesque.
A sans serif typeface.
Distorted and unnatural in shape or size; abnormal and hideous.
Disgusting or otherwise viscerally revolting.
Sans serif.
The belief in a single deity (one god or goddess); especially within an organized religion.
A large Arctic marine mammal related to seals and having long tusks, tough, wrinkled skin, and four flippers, Odobenus rosmarus.
A sharp blow with something hard.
definitions.example: The teacher gave the wayward pupil a rap across the knuckles with her ruler.

definitions.definition: Blame for something.
definitions.example: You can't act irresponsibly and then expect me to take the rap.

definitions.definition: A charge, whether or not it results in a conviction.
A casual talk.
Rap music.
A song, verse, or instance of singing in the style of rap music.
An appraisal.
definitions.example: a good/great/bad rap

definitions.definition: A positive appraisal; a recommendation.
definitions.example: He gave the novel quite a rap.

definitions.definition: A lay or skein containing 120 yards of yarn.
Any of the tokens that passed current for a halfpenny in Ireland in the early part of the eighteenth century; any coin of trifling value.
A whit; a jot.
definitions.example: I don't care a rap.

definitions.definition: To enclose (an object) completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.
To enclose or coil around an object or organism, as a form of grasping.
definitions.example: A snake wraps itself around its prey.

definitions.definition: To conceal by enveloping or enfolding; to hide.
(video production) To finish shooting (filming) a video, television show, or movie.
(lines, words, text, etc.) To break a continuous line (of text) onto the next line
definitions.example: I wrapped the text so that I wouldn't need to scroll to the right to read it.

definitions.definition: To make functionality available through a software wrapper.
To (cause to) reset to an original value after passing a maximum.
definitions.example: The row counter wraps back to zero when no more rows can be inserted.

definitions.definition: A garment that one wraps around the body to keep oneself warm.
A type of food consisting of various ingredients wrapped in a tortilla or pancake.
(entertainment) The completion of all or a major part of a performance.
A wraparound mortgage.
The unwavering strength of will to carry out one's wishes.
To blow air through a wind instrument or horn to make a sound.
To cause (someone) to become breathless, as by a blow to the abdomen, or by physical exertion, running, etc.
definitions.example: The boxer was winded during round two.

definitions.definition: To cause a baby to bring up wind by patting its back after being fed.
To turn a boat or ship around, so that the wind strikes it on the opposite side.
To expose to the wind; to winnow; to ventilate.
To perceive or follow by scent.
definitions.example: The hounds winded the game.

definitions.definition: To rest (a horse, etc.) in order to allow the breath to be recovered; to breathe.
To turn a windmill so that its sails face into the wind.
To turn coils of (a cord or something similar) around something.
definitions.example: to wind thread on a spool or into a ball

definitions.definition: To tighten the spring of a clockwork mechanism such as that of a clock.
definitions.example: Please wind that old-fashioned alarm clock.

definitions.definition: To entwist; to enfold; to encircle.
To travel in a way that is not straight.
definitions.example: Vines wind round a pole.  The river winds through the plain.

definitions.definition: To have complete control over; to turn and bend at one's pleasure; to vary or alter or will; to regulate; to govern.
To introduce by insinuation; to insinuate.
To cover or surround with something coiled about.
definitions.example: to wind a rope with twine

definitions.definition: To cause to move by exerting a winding force; to haul or hoist, as by a winch.
To turn (a ship) around, end for end.
Out of breath
definitions.example: She was winded from her long run.

definitions.definition: To satisfy, carry out, bring to completion (an obligation, a requirement, etc.).
definitions.example: You made a promise, son, and now you must fulfill it.

definitions.definition: To emotionally or artistically satisfy; to develop one's gifts to the fullest.
definitions.example: This is the most fulfilling work I've ever done.

definitions.definition: To obey, follow, comply with (a rule, requirement etc.).

definitions.definition: To package, distribute, or ship goods.
To fill full; fill to the utmost capacity; fill up.
definitions.example: My lady is positively fulfilled of grace.

definitions.definition: Fulfilment
Which causes fulfilment; emotionally or artistically satisfying.
One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.

definitions.definition: A small part of the whole.
definitions.example: an element of doubt;  an element of the picture

definitions.definition: The sky.
(with "the") Atmospheric forces such as strong winds and rains.

definitions.definition: A place or state of being that an individual or object is best suited to.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) The bread and wine taken at Holy Communion.
A group of people within a larger group having a particular common characteristic.
definitions.example: You sometimes find the hooligan element at football matches.

definitions.definition: A component in electrical equipment, often in the form of a coil, having a high resistance, thereby generating heat when a current is passed through it.
definitions.example: The element in this electric kettle can heat the water in under a minute.

definitions.definition: One of the conceptual objects in a markup language, usually represented in text by tags.
Outdoor weather, such as wind or rain.
The basic tenets of an area of knowledge.
The bread and wine of the Eucharist.
A small piece of woodland or a woody hill; a copse.
The lair of an animal, especially of an otter.
(Baltimore) An arrest based on weak evidence intended to demean or punish the subject.
To defeat or reduce the power, independence, or pride of
To make humble or lowly; to make less proud or arrogant; to make meek and submissive.
Not pretentious or magnificent; unpretending; unassuming.

definitions.definition: Having a low opinion of oneself; not proud, arrogant, or assuming; modest.
Near the ground.
A tall, square, tapered, stone monolith topped with a pyramidal point, frequently used as a monument.
(of a dragonfly) To adopt the obelisk posture; to point the tip of the abdomen towards the sun.

A symbol resembling a horizontal line (–), sometimes together with one or two dots (for example, ⨪ or ÷), which was used in ancient manuscripts and texts to mark a word or passage as doubtful or spurious, or redundant; an obelisk.

A dagger symbol (†), which is used in printed matter as a reference mark to refer the reader to a footnote, marginal note, etc.; beside a person's name to indicate that the person is deceased; or beside a date to indicate that it is a person's death date; an obelisk.
A doctor or scientist who specializes in oncology.
An original form or object which is a basis for other forms or objects (particularly manufactured items), or for its generalizations and models.
An early sample or model built to test a concept or process.

definitions.definition: A declaration of a function that specifies the name, return type, and parameters, but none of the body or actual code.
An instance of a category or a concept that combines its most representative attributes.
definitions.example: A robin is a prototype of a bird; a penguin is not.

definitions.definition: A type of race car, a racing sports car not based on a production car. A 4-wheeled cockpit-seating car built especially for racing on sports car circuits, that does not use the silhouette related to a consumer road car.
An alcoholic, often sparkling (carbonated) beverage made from fermented apples; hard cider; apple cider

A non-alcoholic still beverage consisting of the juice of early-harvest apples, usually unfiltered and still containing pulp; apple cider; sweet cider (without pulp such a beverage is called apple juice).
definitions.example: She liked an aged cider. He liked a harder cider.

definitions.definition: A non-alcoholic carbonated beverage made from apples.
(in Korea) A non-alcoholic drink, normally carbonated; equivalent to soft drink.
A cup, glass or serving of any of these beverages.
(usually of relative importance, of degree, quantity or measure) In a double manner; twice the severity or degree.
definitions.example: My mother was always doubly careful when winding the grandfather clock.

definitions.definition: In two ways
definitions.example: Stealing and then lying about it is doubly wrong.

definitions.definition: With duplicity
A subject of a talk or an artistic piece; a topic.
A recurring idea; a motif.
An essay written for school.
The main melody of a piece of music, especially one that is the source of variations.
A song, or a snippet of a song, that identifies a film, a TV program, a character, etc. by playing at the appropriate time.
The collection of color schemes, sounds, artwork etc., that "skin" an environment towards a particular motif.
(grammar) The stem of a word.
Thematic relation of a noun phrase to a verb.
Theta role in generative grammar and government and binding theory.
Topic, what is generally being talked about, as opposed to rheme.
A regional unit of organisation in the Byzantine empire.
To give a theme to.

The state of being loyal; fidelity.
Faithfulness or devotion to some person, cause or nation.
The system of writing Japanese using Chinese characters.
Any individual Chinese character as used in the Japanese language.

definitions.definition: To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.

definitions.definition: To make a sharp jerking movement of the leg, as to strike something.

definitions.definition: To direct to a particular place by a blow with the foot or leg.

definitions.definition: (with "off" or "out") To eject summarily.
To forcibly remove a participant from an online activity.

To attack (a piece) in order to force it to move.

definitions.definition: To show opposition or resistance.
To work a press by impact of the foot on a treadle.
To die.
The action of the verb to kick.

definitions.definition: Alive, active (especially in the phrase alive and kicking).

definitions.definition: (of a party or event) Actively ongoing and enjoyable.
Sudden fatigue as a result of glycogen depletion from not having taken in enough nutrition.
An abrupt rapping sound, as from an impact of a hard object against wood.

definitions.definition: A sharp impact.

definitions.definition: Criticism.
Preignition, a type of abnormal combustion occurring in spark ignition engines caused by self-ignition; also, the characteristic knocking sound associated with it.
A batsman's innings.

definitions.definition: To strike for admittance; to rap upon, as a door.
To criticize verbally; to denigrate; to undervalue.

definitions.definition: To kick a ball towards another player; to pass.
To impress forcibly or strongly; to astonish; to move to admiration or applause.

definitions.definition: Having lots of juice.

definitions.definition: (of a blow, strike, etc.) Strong, painful.
Voluptuous, curvy, thick.

A web browser.

Usually preceded by forms of to give: a slight hint, implication, or suggestion given.
Often preceded by forms of to get or to have: an imprecise idea or slight knowledge of something; a suspicion.
A desire, an inclination.
Fake; not real

A puzzling circumstance.
Objection.

(of a person or an animal) Difficult to train or guide; unruly.
Causing a problem; problematic; troublesome.
Official designation for currency in some parts of the world, including Canada, the United States, Australia, New Zealand, Hong Kong, and elsewhere. Its symbol is $.
(by extension) Money generally.
A quarter of a pound or one crown, historically minted as a coin of approximately the same size and composition as a then-contemporary dollar coin of the United States, and worth slightly more.
Imported from the United States, and paid for in U.S. dollars. (Note: distinguish "dollar wheat", North American farmers' slogan, meaning a market price of one dollar per bushel.)
Any substance that is secreted by an organism
The act of secreting a substance, especially from a gland
A man, a fellow; an ordinary man, a man on the street.
A man who behaves in a particularly laddish or overtly heterosexual manner.
(A lower deck term for) the Captain or Executive Officer of a warship, with particular reference to discipline and punishment.
An exemplar of a certain masculine, independent male archetype.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.

To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.

To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.

(of land) Uneven.

Anything that similarly stores or houses something.
The contents of a wardrobe: an individual's entire collection of clothing.
Any collection of clothing.
Any collection of anything.

A person with authority; authority, ordinance.
Something ordinary or regular.

Being part of the natural order of things; normal, customary, routine.

definitions.definition: Having no special characteristics or function; everyday, common, mundane; often deprecatory.

definitions.definition: Bad or undesirable.
A naval officer of the highest rank; the commander of a country's naval forces.
A naval officer of high rank, immediately below Admiral of the Fleet; the commander of a fleet or squadron.

A flag officer in the United States Navy or Coast Guard of a grade superior to vice admiral and junior to admiral of the fleet (when that grade is used). An admiral is equal in grade or rank to a four-star general.
The ship which carries the admiral, the flagship; also, the most considerable ship of a fleet.
A prince or Saracen leader under the Sultan.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies of the genera Kaniska and Vanessa, especially a red admiral or white admiral.
Relating to or being the second of two items.
Near (or nearer) to the end.
In the past, but close (or closer) to the present time.
A South American mammal of the camel family, Lama glama, used as a domestic beast of burden and a source of wool and meat.
Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.
A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.
A short length of electrical conductor, to make a temporary connection. Also jump wire.
A removable connecting pin on an electronic circuit board.

A long drilling tool used by masons and quarry workers, consisting of an iron bar with a chisel-edged steel tip at one or both ends, operated by striking it against the rock, turning it slightly with each blow.
A crude kind of sleigh, usually a simple box on runners which are in one piece with the poles that form the thills.
A jumping spider
The larva of the cheese fly.
(18th century) One of certain Calvinistic Methodists in Wales whose worship was characterized by violent convulsions.
A spring to impel the star wheel, or a pawl to lock fast a wheel, in a repeating timepiece.
A shot in which the player releases the ball at the highest point of a jump; a jump shot.

To connect with an electrical jumper.

A period of 4.32 billion years (1000 yugas).

Traditionally the fourth of the four seasons, typically regarded as being from December 23 to March 20 in continental regions of the Northern Hemisphere or the months of June, July and August in the Southern Hemisphere. It is the time when the sun is lowest in the sky, resulting in short days, and the time of year with the lowest atmospheric temperatures for the region.
The period of decay, old age, death, or the like.
Someone with dark skin, eyes and hair, seen as best suited to certain colors of clothing.

The act of improving; advancement or growth; a bettering

The state of being improved; betterment; advance
Something which is improved
definitions.example: the new edition is an improvement on the old.

definitions.definition: Increase; growth; progress; advance.
(in plural) Valuable additions or betterments, for example buildings, clearings, drains, fences, etc., on premises.
(Patent Laws): A useful addition to, or modification of, a machine, manufacture, or composition.
Our universe, the sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: Powerful telescopes look far back into the distant reaches of the Universe.

definitions.definition: The sum of everything that exists in the cosmos, including time and space itself.
definitions.example: I think that the universe was created by a life force rather than a deity.

definitions.definition: An entity similar to our universe; one component of a larger entity known as the multiverse.
Everything under consideration.
definitions.example: In all this universe of possibilities, there is only one feasible option.

definitions.definition: A sample taken from the population.
An imaginary collection of worlds.
definitions.example: The universe in this comic book series is richly imagined.

definitions.definition: (films) A collection of stories with characters and settings that are less interrelated than those of sequels or prequels.
A whole world, in the sense of perspective or social setting.
definitions.example: That didn’t just rock my world, it rocked my universe.

definitions.definition: A sterol lipid synthesized by the liver and transported in the bloodstream to the membranes of all animal cells; it plays a central role in many biochemical processes and, as a lipoprotein that coats the walls of blood vessels, is associated with cardiovascular disease.
The level of cholesterol in the body.
definitions.example: improve your cholesterol; lower your cholesterol

definitions.definition: Various sounds, usually unwanted or unpleasant.
definitions.example: He knew that it was trash day, when the garbage collectors made all the noise.

definitions.definition: Sound or signal generated by random fluctuations.
Unwanted part of a signal.
(by extension) Unwanted fuss or bustle; useless activity.
The measured level of variation in gene expression among cells, regardless of source, within a supposedly identical population.
Rumour or complaint.
definitions.example: The problems with the new computer system are causing a lot of noise at Head Office.

definitions.definition: Music, in general; a concert; also, a company of musicians; a band.
A genre of rock music that uses static and other non-musical sounds, also influenced by art rock.
To make a noise; to sound.
To spread news of; to spread as rumor or gossip.
To begin, commence, initiate.
To begin an activity.
definitions.example: The rain started at 9:00.

definitions.definition: To have its origin (at), begin.
definitions.example: The blue line starts one foot away from the wall.

definitions.definition: To startle or be startled; to move or be moved suddenly.
To break away, to come loose.
To put into play.
To pour out; to empty; to tap and begin drawing from.
definitions.example: to start a water cask

definitions.definition: To start one's periods (menstruation).
definitions.example: Have you started yet?

definitions.definition: The act of something that starts.
definitions.example: constant startings and stoppings

definitions.definition: One who sweeps floors or chimneys.
A detector (for mines).
Any of the small, tropical marine perciform fishes of the family Pempheridae, typically with deeply keeled, compressed bodies and large eyes.
A defender who is the last line of defence before the goalkeeper.
A person who sweeps the ice ahead of the rock in play.
A batsman who plays sweep shots.
A fielding position along the boundary; a fielder in this position.
A tree that has fallen over a river with branches extending into the water.
definitions.example: ..tree will be hanging over the water about canoe level (a sweeper).. Basic Essentials Canoe Paddling, 3rd - Page 62

definitions.definition: A carpet sweeper.
(including) A vacuum cleaner.
(including, Cebu) A group of students tasked at cleaning the homeroom after class dismissal.
A closed container for liquids or gases.
An open container or pool for storing water or other liquids.
A pond, pool, or small lake, natural or artificial.
The fuel reservoir of a vehicle.
The amount held by a container; a tankful.
definitions.example: I burned three tanks of gas on the drive to New York.

definitions.definition: An armoured fighting vehicle, armed with a gun, and moving on caterpillar tracks.
(Australian and Indian English) A reservoir or dam.
A large metal container for holding drinking water for animals, usually placed near a wind-driven water pump, in an animal pen or field.
By extension a small pond for the same purpose.
A very muscular and physically imposing person. Somebody who is built like a tank.
A unit or character designed primarily around damage absorption and holding the attention of the enemy (as opposed to dealing damage, healing, or other tasks).
A prison cell, or prison generally.
A metaphorical place where a player goes to contemplate a decision; see in the tank.
To fail or fall (often used in describing the economy or the stock market); to degenerate or decline rapidly; to plummet.
To attract the attacks of an enemy target in cooperative team-based combat, so that one's teammates can defeat the enemy in question more efficiently.
To put (fuel, etc.) into a tank.
To deliberately lose a sports match with the intent of gaining a perceived future competitive advantage.
To resist damage; to be attacked without being hurt.
To contemplate a decision for a long time; to go in the tank.
To be very happy, be delighted, exult; to feel joy.
To have (someone) as a lover or spouse; to enjoy sexually.
To make happy, exhilarate.
To enjoy.
Exciting wonder or surprise; astonishing; wonderful.
definitions.example: I went to a marvellous party last week.

definitions.definition: A publication that is published once a month.
The menstrual period.
Occurring every month.
Every month.
An element of personal character that enables one to distinguish the wise from the unwise.
A piece of wise advice.
The discretionary use of knowledge for the greatest good.
The ability to apply relevant knowledge in an insightful way, especially to different situations from that in which the knowledge was gained.
The ability to make a decision based on the combination of knowledge, experience, and intuitive understanding.
The ability to know and apply spiritual truths.
An imaginary or real place full of wonder or marvels.

Any substance or drug that reduces pain, induces sleep and may alter mood or behaviour; in some contexts, especially in reference to the opiates-and-opioids class, especially in reference to illegal drugs, and often both.
Any type of numbing drug.
(of a person) The quality of speaking or stating the truth; truthfulness.
Something that is true; a truthful statement; a truth.
Agreement with the facts; accordance with the truth; accuracy or precision.
Act of being exact and accurate.
Correctness and carefulness in one's plan of action.
More than half (50%) of some group.
definitions.example: The majority agreed that the new proposal was the best.

definitions.definition: The difference between the winning vote and the rest of the votes.
definitions.example: The winner with 53% had a 6% majority over the loser with 47%.

definitions.definition: Legal adulthood.
definitions.example: By the time I reached my majority, I had already been around the world twice.

definitions.definition: The office held by a member of the armed forces in the rank of major.
definitions.example: On receiving the news of his promotion, Charles Snodgrass said he was delighted to be entering his majority.

definitions.definition: Ancestors; ancestry.

Any of various parrots of the genera Ara, Anodorhynchus, Cyanopsitta, Orthopsittaca, Primolius and Diopsittaca of Central and South America, including the largest parrots and characterized by long sabre-shaped tails, curved powerful bills, and usually brilliant plumage.
